predicate.statement: All documents submitted with the application must be in English or Welsh; if not, the applicant must provide the original document together with a full, independently verified translation.

predicate.statement: If the applicant has, in the 12 months before the date of application, received an award from a government or international scholarship agency covering both fees and living costs for study in the UK, they must have provided written consent to the application from that government or agency.

predicate.statement: An applicant in the UK cannot switch into the Innovator Founder route if they have, or were last granted, permission as a Visitor, Short-term Student, Parent of a Child Student, Seasonal Worker, domestic worker in a private household, or outside the Immigration Rules.

prohibited_categories: domestic worker in a private household

applicant: Not be in or last granted one of the prohibited permission categories when switching in-country.
caseworker: Check the applicant's current or most recent permission category against the prohibited list.

anchor.verbatim_text: An applicant who is in the UK cannot apply to switch into the Innovator Founder route if they have, or were last granted, permission as any of the following: as a Visitor, as a Short-term Student, as a Parent of a Child Student, as a Seasonal Worker, as a domestic worker in a private household, outside the Immigration Rules

predicate.statement: An applicant switching from Student permission must have completed the course of study for which their CAS was assigned (or a course to which paragraph ST 27.3 of Appendix Student applies), or, if the course was leading to a PhD award, must have completed at least 12 months of that course.

options.statement: The applicant has completed the course of study for which the CAS was assigned.
The course is one to which paragraph ST 27.3 of Appendix Student applies and has been completed.
The course was leading to a PhD award and the applicant has completed at least 12 months of that course.

applicant: Demonstrate completion of the CAS course, or completion of at least 12 months of a PhD course.
caseworker: Consider supporting evidence such as a results transcript or Student Sponsor notification where the CAS end date suggests the course is not yet complete.
Where the CAS course is at PhD level, use the course start date to assess whether at least 12 months have been completed.
If required, write to the applicant using the Validity reminder template before rejecting the application as invalid.

anchor.verbatim_text: An applicant who is applying for permission to stay and has, or last had, permission as a student must have completed the course of study for which the Confirmation of Acceptance for Studies was assigned (or a course to which paragraph ST 27.3 of Appendix Student applies). Alternatively, if the course was leading to a PhD award they must have completed at least 12 months of that course.
